top_sources.top_context: upports healthy aging. This is why it’s being called an “exercise pill” by many researchers. ——————————————————————————— **Clinical Results** The most compelling evidence so far comes from the Phase IIa study in 65 patients with type 2 diabetes on metformin. After 28 days of 1000mg/day ATX-304: * **Fasting plasma glucose dropped by –0.60 mM vs –0.10 mM with placebo** (~11 mg/dL greater reduction, p=0.01). * **Insulin resistance (HOMA-IR) improved by a statistically significant margin** in the treatment group (p=0.0097). * **Diastolic blood pressure decreased significantly**, wit

top_sources.top_context: mpound** (not aggressively cycled) ### Summary - **SLU = powerful but cyclical** - **ATX = sustainable + longevity-focused** ## ⭐ DOSING — Hunter's Stack Protocol ### Standalone ranges - **SLU-PP-332:** 250-1000 mcg/day (research range). **Start LOW + go SLOW** — too high builds up ROS fast. - **ATX-304:** 50-300 mg/day orally typical. One trial used 1000 mg/day with no adverse effects. Hunter likes **200-400 mg/day**. ### ⭐ Stacking Recommendation - **SLU-PP-332: 250–500 mcg/day** - **ATX-304: 200–400 mg/day** Keeps you on "exercise mimetic" side of curve, NOT "too much metabolic stress" side.

taylor-williams.female_specific_note: Taylor Williams Coaching focuses on female-specific HRT/peptide guidance. Per-peptide notes here often emphasize female dosing, fertility, hormone interactions.

reset_biology_synthesis.recommendation: Reset Biology suggests asking the practitioner to consider ATX-304 as a sustainable AMPK activator framed by Hunter as an oral exercise mimetic, with the operative ceiling anchored to the published trial — Hunter email threadId 199789ab3211cefa (2025-09-23): "In clinical trials, ATX-304 was given as a 1000mg oral suspension once daily," while Hunter's stack guidance is lower: Hunter email threadId 19a9e63d0239bb68 (2025-11-19): "ATX-304: 50-300 mg/day orally typical. One trial used 1000 mg/day with no adverse effects. Hunter likes 200-400 mg/day." We'd ask the prescribing physician to weigh dose-responsiveness because Taylor (G27dIPBJUSs 00:57:51.640): "I noticed outsized benefits at like 5 to 700 mg per day. That's where it really starts to hit," and to weigh patient candidacy because Hunter email threadId 199789ab3211cefa (2025-09-23) frames the fit as "People struggling with type 2 diabetes or prediabetes" and "Individuals with fatty liver or early signs of metabolic dysfunction."
reset_biology_synthesis.caveats:
- No Trevor Bachmeyer signal on ATX-304 in current corpus — synthesis is Hunter (emails) + Taylor (podcast) only.
- Dose-response is real and asymmetric. Hunter email threadId 199789ab3211cefa (2025-09-23): "For most people, the sweet spot will be around 200mg/day, but dosing may depend on body weight and clinical goals." Co-host on Taylor's show (G27dIPBJUSs 00:35:04.960): "But you take 200 milligrams, 300 milligrams, you're probably not even going to feel anything at all." Two practitioners voicing opposite views of the same dose band — practitioner should resolve before dispensing.
- Cost / access caveat from Taylor's show (JccJTHbWF84 00:34:42.320): "is it worth 1,200 bucks a month to have for most people? No." — therapeutic threshold (5–700 mg/day) puts the cost burden high.
- AMPK-activator class caution: Hunter email threadId 19a9e63d0239bb68 (2025-11-19): "ATX-304 is dose-responsive. Do NOT mega-dose AMPK activators (like you wouldn't mega-dose metformin)."
- Mitochondrial-overspin caution: Hunter email threadId 19a9e63d0239bb68 (2025-11-19): "Too aggressive ERR activation → mitochondrial stress, excessive fatty-acid oxidation, unintended muscle remodeling." Note: this caveat is framed in the SLU-PP-332 column of the table; ATX is positioned as the more sustainable side of the stack.
- Empty-stomach timing only stated in Hunter email threadId 199789ab3211cefa (2025-09-23): "Best taken first thing in the morning on an empty stomach, to mimic the natural fasting-induced AMPK activation."
- Phase IIa human trial was 28 days only — Hunter email threadId 199789ab3211cefa (2025-09-23): "the Phase IIa study in 65 patients with type 2 diabetes on metformin. After 28 days of 1000mg/day ATX-304" — long-duration human data not yet established.
- No baseline_protocol from the 2023 Hunter/Jay Campbell cheat sheet (peptide post-dates that document).
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_consensus: Two-expert agreement (Hunter + Taylor); Trevor is silent. Both agree ATX-304 is the dosed-up exercise-mimetic of choice over SLU-PP-332 ('Sloop'). Taylor (JccJTHbWF84 00:33:30.559): "I love it. I love that molecule so much," and (JccJTHbWF84 00:33:47.679): "I would pick the ATX. Like I feel the best when I'm on that." Taylor (G27dIPBJUSs 00:57:30.080): "ATX 304 is more effective than Sloop. Sloop activates a little quicker and lasts a little longer, but ATX definitely allows me to push myself harder without fatigue." Hunter email threadId 19a9e63d0239bb68 (2025-11-19): "SLU = powerful but cyclical" and "ATX = sustainable + longevity-focused." Both agree the trial dose was 1,000 mg/day with clean safety: Taylor co-host (JccJTHbWF84 00:34:51.679): "In the the human studies that were used on it, they used 1,000 milligrams a day and they had only positive health benefits. So no adverse effects, no increase in resting heart rate, only benefit in cholesterol profile, only benefit in reduction in inflammation, benefit in body composition." Hunter email threadId 199789ab3211cefa (2025-09-23): "After 28 days of 1000mg/day ATX-304" with "no serious adverse events or safety issues were reported." Both also agree that consumer 100 mg capsules force users into dose stacks: Hunter email threadId 199789ab3211cefa (2025-09-23): "Capsules are typically standardized to 100mg each" and Taylor's co-host (G27dIPBJUSs 00:57:48.520): "Most of the dosing you're going to see from people is 100 mg capsules."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_disagreement: Effective dose floor differs. Hunter email threadId 199789ab3211cefa (2025-09-23): "For most people, the sweet spot will be around 200mg/day," and Hunter email threadId 19a9e63d0239bb68 (2025-11-19): "Hunter likes 200-400 mg/day." Taylor (G27dIPBJUSs 00:57:51.640): "I noticed outsized benefits at like 5 to 700 mg per day. That's where it really starts to hit," and Taylor's co-host (JccJTHbWF84 00:35:04.960): "But you take 200 milligrams, 300 milligrams, you're probably not even going to feel anything at all." Net: Hunter writes 200 mg as a sweet spot; Taylor's table reports the felt threshold at 500–700 mg. NO AVERAGING — surface both. Newer Hunter content also widens his own range: 2025-09-23 Hunter writes "100–300mg per day range" as the effective band, then 2025-11-19 (newer) Hunter writes "50-300 mg/day orally typical" with personal preference "200-400 mg/day." The 2025-11-19 standalone-range guidance is the operative recommendation per newer-overrides-older; both dates carry. Manufacturing-vs-trial gap: Taylor (G27dIPBJUSs 00:58:09.359): "ATX. Yeah, probably like 250 or 500 mg" — argues vendors should have built higher-dose capsules, while Hunter holds 100 mg standard.

chronic.dose: Hunter email threadId 19a9e63d0239bb68 (2025-11-19): "ATX-304: 50-300 mg/day orally typical. One trial used 1000 mg/day with no adverse effects. Hunter likes 200-400 mg/day." Hunter email threadId 199789ab3211cefa (2025-09-23): "For most people, the sweet spot will be around 200mg/day, but dosing may depend on body weight and clinical goals." Taylor (G27dIPBJUSs 00:57:51.640): "I noticed outsized benefits at like 5 to 700 mg per day. That's where it really starts to hit."
chronic.route: Hunter email threadId 199789ab3211cefa (2025-09-23): "In clinical trials, ATX-304 was given as a 1000mg oral suspension once daily. However, new capsule formulations are being developed for easier use." Hunter email threadId 19a9e63d0239bb68 (2025-11-19): "50-300 mg/day orally typical."
chronic.frequency: Hunter email threadId 199789ab3211cefa (2025-09-23): "Take 1–3 capsules daily (100–300mg). Best taken first thing in the morning on an empty stomach, to mimic the natural fasting-induced AMPK activation."
chronic.duration: Hunter email threadId 19a9e63d0239bb68 (2025-11-19): "AMPK activation = one of most consistently validated longevity pathways across species. Can be a long-term metabolic + healthspan compound (not aggressively cycled)." Phase IIa human trial duration was 28 days per Hunter email threadId 199789ab3211cefa (2025-09-23): "After 28 days of 1000mg/day ATX-304."
chronic.verbatim_citation: Hunter email threadId 199789ab3211cefa (2025-09-23): "the effective (and affordable) dose for humans is expected to be in the 100–300mg per day range." Hunter email threadId 19a9e63d0239bb68 (2025-11-19): "Hunter likes 200-400 mg/day."
